justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical, jsonwebtoken CVEs flagged as Critical with specific CVE numbers, 5/5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, crypto-random-string, left-pad, moment), safe_to_deploy is false, severity ratings are differentiated (Critical for stripe/jwt/crypto-random-string, Important for moment/left-pad).

observations.text: All 5 expected dependency risks identified with accurate technical details
Stripe wildcard correctly classified as Critical with concrete reproduction scenario
jsonwebtoken flagged with specific CVE numbers (CVE-2022-23529, CVE-2022-23539/23540)
crypto-random-string ESM-only issue correctly identified with ERR_REQUIRE_ESM detail
left-pad supply chain risk noted with reference to 2016 incident and native alternative
moment.js deprecation identified with replacement suggestions
Bonus findings: pg version range concern and missing lockfile/engines field
moment.js classified as Important rather than Suggestion — slightly over-weighted but not harmful
Actionable fixes provided for every finding with time estimates

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical, jsonwebtoken CVEs flagged as Critical with specific CVE numbers, all 5 of 5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, crypto-random-string, left-pad, moment), safe_to_deploy is false, and severities are differentiated (Critical for stripe/jsonwebtoken/crypto-random-string, Important for moment/left-pad/pg).

observations.text: Stripe wildcard correctly identified as Critical with detailed reproduction steps showing how breaking changes propagate
jsonwebtoken CVEs cited by number (CVE-2022-23529, CVE-2022-23539, CVE-2022-23540) with CVSS score and attack vector
crypto-random-string ESM incompatibility identified with correct error message and fix options
left-pad supply chain risk noted with historical context and native alternative
moment.js deprecation flagged appropriately as Important rather than Critical
Bonus findings: pg version range concern, missing lockfile, missing engine constraints — shows thoroughness beyond rubric requirements
Confidence section properly caveats CJS assumption for crypto-random-string
crypto-random-string rated Critical rather than Important — the rubric expected Important since it's a runtime error (detectable on deploy) rather than a silent failure like the stripe wildcard

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: stripe wildcard identified as Critical, jsonwebtoken CVEs flagged as Critical, all 5/5 risks identified (stripe, jsonwebtoken, crypto-random-string, left-pad, moment), safe_to_deploy is false, severity ratings are differentiated (Critical/Important/Suggestion levels).

observations.text: All five expected dependency risks identified with accurate technical detail
Stripe wildcard correctly classified as Critical with clear explanation of payment risk
jsonwebtoken CVEs cited by number (CVE-2022-23529, CVE-2022-23539/23540/23541) — excellent specificity
crypto-random-string ESM issue correctly identified with ERR_REQUIRE_ESM explanation
left-pad supply chain risk and moment.js deprecation both caught
Goes beyond rubric with bonus findings: missing lockfile, missing security hardening deps, no observability
crypto-random-string rated Critical rather than Important — the rubric expected Important, though the agent's reasoning (runtime crash in payment service) is defensible
moment and left-pad placed in important_issues rather than moment as Suggestion — minor severity inflation but both were identified
Reproduction steps provided for each critical finding, making issues actionable
Confidence section properly notes the CJS assumption as low-confidence area
